data.title: Hitachi GAAP EPS of ¥176.76, revenue of ¥10,586.7B
data.description:
• Hitachi press release (HTHIY): FY GAAP EPS of ¥176.76.
  • Revenue of ¥10,586.7B.

data.title: Allied Gold falls amid attack in Mali on Saturday
data.description:
Allied Gold (AAUC), which is being acquired by China's Zijin Mining (ZIJMF) (ZIJMY), dropped 4% as Mali's defense minister was killed in a suicide attack by an al-Qaeda affiliate on Saturday.

Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in an attack on his residence, according to media accounts. 

Allied Gold (AAUC) operates a portfolio of three producing assets and development projects located in Côte d'Ivoire, Mali, and Ethiopia.

Zijin Mining (ZIJMF) (ZIJMY) agreed to buy Allied Gold (AAUC) for ~C$5.5B (US$4B) in cash in January.

data.title: Norwood Financial anticipates $2.2M in 2026 margin accretion while integrating Presence Bank
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Norwood Financial Corp. (NWFL) Q1 2026

Management view

  • "We began 2026 with strong performance, extending the momentum we began to build last year. This is the first quarter that included results from the Presence Bank acquisition, increasing our assets, loan portfolio, geographic presence and earnings power." (President, CEO & Director James Donnelly)

  • "Net interest income was a record $24,600,000, an increase of 38% compared with the first quarter of 2025. Net interest income margin expanded by 38 basis points to 3.68%." (President, CEO & Director James Donnelly)

  • "I am pleased to report that we are on plan with these activities." (President, CEO & Director James Donnelly)

  • "Among our early accomplishments is the completion of our core integration, unifying our IT and HR systems." (President, CEO & Director James Donnelly)

  • "One item I am really excited about is the commercial credit system, which we will integrate in July. This uses embedded AI and machine learning to enhance the productivity of our talented credit officers by bringing automation, speed and quality to the process." (President, CEO & Director James Donnelly)

  • "One demonstration of this is that we now expect accretion to shareholder value ahead of our original projections." (President, CEO & Director James Donnelly)

  • "We had about $5 million in merger charges in the quarter." (Executive VP & CFO John McCaffery)

  • "Quarterly expenses were up as a percent of average assets compared to Q4 2025. Most of this increase is in technology related." (Executive VP & CFO John McCaffery)

Outlook

  • Management did not provide formal EPS or revenue guidance in the transcript; forward-looking commentary centered on margin, expense run-rate, integration timing, and purchase-accounting accretion.

  • "So on a go-forward basis for the full year of 2026, so we're scheduled at about $2.2 million for 2026, dropping to about $2 million for 2027 in total margin accretion." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • "I think the margin can increase throughout the year. I wouldn't put it at 8 basis points on a linked quarter basis, but maybe 3 to 4, 5 basis points." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • "I think for OpEx going forward, that -- the level that we're at is probably a pretty good run rate." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

Financial results

  • "Net interest income was a record $24,600,000." (President, CEO & Director Donnelly)

  • "We achieved record net interest income increasing $3.6 million on a linked quarter basis due to higher interest-earning assets." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • "Margin improved 8 basis points due to a slight decline in deposit costs, coupled with a 7 basis point increase in interest-earning asset yields." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • "Our coverage ratio stands at 1.09% compared to 1.07% at year-end." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • "To expand on Jim's point earlier, growth since January 5, loans grew approximately $46 million or 8.4% annualized and deposits grew about $70 million or $11.6 million on an annualized basis." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

Q&A

  • Daniel Cardenas, Brean Capital, LLC, Research Division: asked how much of the quarter’s operating expense increase was tech-related and what a good forward run-rate is; Executive VP & CFO McCaffery said investments included "the Abrigo system and our new accounting system" and added, "I think for OpEx going forward, that -- the level that we're at is probably a pretty good run rate," while also stating, "I wouldn't drop it more than below [ $15.8 million ], I think, for the quarter."

  • Cardenas, Brean Capital, LLC, Research Division: asked about purchase accounting/yield accretion contribution and its go-forward impact; Executive VP & CFO McCaffery answered, "the total pretax impact of purchase accounting was 4.35%" and added, "we're scheduled at about $2.2 million for 2026, dropping to about $2 million for 2027 in total margin accretion."

  • Cardenas, Brean Capital, LLC, Research Division: asked whether nonperformers were driven by the acquisition and what drove the increase; Executive VP & CFO McCaffery said, "I don't think they contribute to any nonperforming from Presence. So that was mostly us," and President, CEO & Director Donnelly added, "It's largely on commercial side."

  • Matthew Breese, Stephens Inc., Research Division: asked about deposit competition, promotional rates, and deposit-cost outlook; Executive VP & CFO McCaffery said, "we are not talking about raising any of our specials on CDs at all" and "we're not seeing competitive pressure in our markets on deposit pricing yet," while President, CEO & Director Donnelly added, "we're not seeing a lot of upward pressure. I'm still seeing some competitors bringing their rates down."

  • Breese, Stephens Inc., Research Division: asked about origination yields/pipeline and margin trajectory; President, CEO & Director Donnelly said, "Pipeline is very healthy" and "the closings that we just had averaged 7.05% for the last $18.5 million we booked," while Executive VP & CFO McCaffery added, "I think the margin can increase throughout the year" and framed potential improvement as "maybe 3 to 4, 5 basis points."

  • Cardenas, Brean Capital, LLC, Research Division: asked what could drive fee income higher beyond Q1; President, CEO & Director Donnelly cited debit and staffing-driven initiatives, saying, "we were an underperformer from debit revenue" and "Treasury management is geared up for the second half of the year should do a nice job as well."

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts appeared slightly skeptical and detail-oriented, with repeated drilling into expense sustainability, purchase accounting/margin components, deposit competition, and credit migration, including questions such as "what's a good run rate on the operating expenses going forward?" (Daniel Cardenas, Brean Capital, LLC, Research Division) and requests for "some color on competitive conditions around deposits" (Matthew Breese, Stephens Inc., Research Division).

  • Management tone was slightly positive in prepared remarks and more measured in Q&A, emphasizing momentum and integration progress while caveating variability in forward margin expansion; examples included "We began 2026 with strong performance" (President, CEO & Director Donnelly) alongside "I wouldn't put it at 8 basis points" (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery).

  • Compared with the prior quarter’s prepared-remarks-only format (no analyst questions), the current quarter carried a more probing, numbers-focused external tone due to the live Q&A, while management continued to emphasize continuity around integration and efficiency initiatives.

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Q1 2026 shifted from announcing/closing the Presence Bank deal (Q4 2025) to reporting early integration execution, including "the completion of our core integration" and brand-unification work. (President, CEO & Director Donnelly)

  • Expense and merger-cost discussion intensified in Q1 2026: management cited "about $5 million in merger charges" and technology-driven expense pressure, versus "about $520,000 in merger charges" referenced in Q4 2025 prepared remarks. (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • Q1 2026 introduced more specific near-term operational timing, including "the commercial credit system, which we will integrate in July" and completion of the core conversion "on April 5," while Q4 2025 framed AI adoption more broadly as part of integration planning. (President, CEO & Director Donnelly; Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • Analyst focus was absent in Q4 2025 ("I am not showing any questions at this time") and became central in Q1 2026, concentrating on expense run-rate, margin durability, deposit competition, and nonperforming assets. (Operator; analysts and management)

Risks and concerns

  • "Below the margin line, our quarterly results do continue to include merger charges." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • "Quarterly expenses were up as a percent of average assets compared to Q4 2025. Most of this increase is in technology related." (Executive VP & CFO McCaffery)

  • "It's largely on commercial side." (President, CEO & Director Donnelly)

  • "I think our ability to squeeze out of the deposits will be smaller than it had been." (President, CEO & Director Donnelly)

Final takeaway

Management described Q1 2026 as the first quarter including Presence Bank, highlighted record net interest income of $24,600,000 and a 3.68% net interest margin, and positioned the integration as tracking to plan with core systems unified. In Q&A, analysts pressed on expense run-rate, margin components (including purchase accounting), deposit pricing competition, and nonperforming trends, while management pointed to ongoing tech investment, targeted operating leverage over time, and scheduled purchase-accounting-related margin accretion of about $2.2 million in 2026 and about $2 million in 2027.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wall Street trades mixed ahead of Big Tech earnings and Fed meeting
data.description:
Wall Street trades mixed on Monday as investors brace for a pivotal week—eyeing Big Tech earnings, the upcoming Federal Reserve decision, and ongoing developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.1%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.1%. 

From a sector-by-sector stance, 4 of the 11 S&P sectors are in the green, with communication services at the top. Going the other way, consumer staples has been the weakest performer. 

Earnings remain in focus this week, with Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta (META) scheduled to report results on Wednesday, followed by Apple (AAPL) the next day. 

Meanwhile, Iran gave the U.S. a new proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, with nuclear negotiations postponed for a later stage, according to Axios. 

“This may be the most important week for markets so far this year, with big tech earnings and a key Federal Reserve meeting coming at a time when markets are trying to figure out their next step now that it seems like the Iran conflict is contained,” Glen Smith, chief investment officer of GDS Wealth Management, stated. 

Additionally, U.S. Treasury yields moved higher. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose to 3.80%, while the 10-year yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.33%, and the 30-year yield (US30Y) advanced to 4.94%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Micron Technology (MU) climbed 4.5%, while shares of Domino's Pizza (DPZ) dropped -9.2%.

data.title: Top ETFs with exposure to Verizon, ranked by Quant ratings
data.description:
Verizon Communications (VZ) is trading more than 2% on Monday, buoyed by first-quarter results that topped profit expectations and delivered a milestone in wireless subscriber growth, reinforcing its position as one of the top-rated telecom stocks on Seeking Alpha's Quant system.

In light of this, below is a list of ETFs with exposure to Verizon, ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. 

The list is topped by iShares US Telecommunications ETF (IYZ) with a Quant Rating of 4.20 and an impressive year-to-date performance of 24.04%. The StSt SPDR S&P US Com Ser Sel Sector UCITS ETF Acc (SSEXF) follows closely behind with a rating of 4.15.

Moving down the rankings, the VanEck Ms Developed Markets Div Lead UCITS ETF (TKCPF) holds a Buy rating of 3.75 with an 8.66% year-to-date return. The Parnassus Value Select ETF (PRVS) marks the transition to Hold ratings at 3.23, followed by several dividend-focused ETFs in the middle and lower tiers of the list.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant system ranks stocks based on their performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Each stock is rated on a scale of 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered bullish and any rating of 2.5 or below considered bearish.

Here is the list:

  1. iShares US Telecommunications ETF (IYZ), Quant Rating: 4.20

  2. StSt SPDR S&P US Com Ser Sel Sector UCITS ETF Acc (SSEXF), Quant Rating: 4.15

  3. VanEck Ms Developed Markets Div Lead UCITS ETF (TKCPF), Quant Rating: 3.75

  4. Parnassus Value Select ETF (PRVS), Quant Rating: 3.23

  5. First Trust S&P 500 Diversified Dv Aristocrats ETF (KNGZ), Quant Rating: 3.04

  6. First Tr Morningstar Dividend Leaders Index Fund ETF (FDL), Quant Rating: 2.96

  7. Invesco Dow Jones Industrial Average Div ETF (DJD), Quant Rating: 2.79

  8. WBI Power Factor High Dividend ETF (WBIY), Quant Rating: 2.54

  9. Polen Dividend Income ETF (DIVZ), Quant Rating: 2.37

  10. FT Vest DJIA Dogs 10 Target Income ETF (DOGG), Quant Rating: 2.21

data.title: AI is the 'sixth major innovation wave' : Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Seth B. Carpenter, Chief Global Economist of Morgan Stanley, characterizes artificial intelligence as the “sixth major innovation wave” since the Industrial Revolution, expressing confidence that “productivity will be substantially boosted over time” even as debates intensify over potential labor market upheaval.

In a research note, Carpenter acknowledges that narratives on AI “range from unbridled optimism to fears of a disruptive force in labor markets,” but contends that history, current data, and economic theory support a more nuanced perspective.

The strategist draws parallels between AI and previous technological transformations, noting that every innovation wave, mechanization, electrification, mass production, automation, and the IT revolution, “has generated anxiety about job loss.”

In each instance, “productivity rose, tasks shifted, and labor demand ultimately expanded,” with technology altering “the composition of work” rather than wholesale eliminating occupations, according to Carpenter. 

Current labor market indicators offer grounds for cautious optimism, Carpenter observes, noting that despite rapid advances in AI compute and increasing firm adoption, aggregate measures show “remarkably little disruption.”

Industries with higher AI exposure have recorded stronger productivity gains “driven primarily by faster output growth rather than fewer hours worked,” Carpenter said. Youth unemployment, frequently cited as evidence of AI-driven displacement, remains largely consistent with “historical cyclical patterns” when adjusted for broader hiring slowdowns.

The compressed timeline of AI adoption presents distinct challenges, as earlier innovation waves “unfolded over decades” while AI adoption has been “much faster,” the strategist warns.

If productivity gains materialize quickly and spread broadly, the result could be “almost recession-like increases in unemployment” until markets adjust, according to Carpenter.

Multiple economic mechanisms could soften potential disruptions, including “demand feedback effects” where productivity-driven income gains support aggregate consumption and wealth effects, Carpenter added. 

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT)

data.title: Hasbro faces union pressure from employees working on Magic: The Gathering Arena
data.description:
Hasbro (HAS) faces unionization pressure after more than 100 developers working on Magic: The Gathering Arena at the toy giant's Wizards of the Coast subsidiary moved to unionize with the Communications Workers of America. The employees indicated that they want more say over corporate decisions and improved working conditions.

In a statement, the workers indicated that they are organizing to gain a voice in layoffs, ensure accountability throughout management, and secure a living wage that allows employees to sustainably build their lives. They also want job protection against generative AI replacing them. While the group has filed for a union election with the National Labor Relations Board, it is asking Hasbro (HAS) to voluntarily recognize the union by May 1.

Magic: The Gathering Arena is a free-to-play digital collectible card game that adapts the original Magic: The Gathering trading card game for PC and mobile platforms.

The push comes as Magic continues to be a major profit driver for Hasbro (HAS), with the franchise generating about $1.72B last year, including roughly $500M in digital gaming revenue at Wizards of the Coast, to which Magic: The Gathering Arena was a significant contributor.

The announcement expands the growing union efforts specifically in the tabletop roleplaying games industry. In June 2023,  Communications Workers of America-represented workers at popular tabletop publisher Paizo ratified their first contract with their employer, securing wage increases, expanded benefits, and enhanced protections for workers. Over 4K workers have organized across the gaming industry to expand game worker solidarity as part of CWA’s Campaign to Organize Digital Employees, spanning from AAA studios in Microsoft's (MSFT) gaming division to indie publishers like Heart Machine, which voluntarily recognized their union last month.

data.title: Visa, Mastercard up for another strong quarter, but cross-border volumes will impact sentiment
data.description:
Two credit card network giants, Visa (V) and Mastercard (MA), are set to report their quarterly financial results this week, and stable consumer spending, despite elevated macro and geopolitical risks, is expected to support overall growth.

"Despite elevated macro and geopolitical noise throughout early 2026, fundamentals across V and MA remain intact, with both continuing to see stable consumer spend trends with diversification supportive of overall growth," said Citi in its research note.

However, the stock price reaction depends on several factors. V and MA are down ~11% year-to-date as investors gauge the fallout of the Iran conflict on volumes and transactions.

San Francisco, California-based Visa will report its fiscal second-quarter earnings tomorrow after market close.

Average analyst estimate for adjusted EPS stands at $3.10, vs. $3.17 in FQ1 and $2.76 in FQ2 of 2025. Net revenue consensus estimate stands at $10.75B, vs. $10.90B recorded in the prior quarter and $9.59B in the same period a year ago.

Last quarter, the stock slid despite earnings and revenue topping the consensus estimate, likely driven by a deceleration in international transaction revenues and longer-term perceived headwinds, according to Evercore ISI.

"With the war in the Middle East having an immediate impact on travel in the region and surrounding areas, we don't expect the color on cross-border to be positive, and that may offset the expected solid quarterly results for some investors," said Evercore analysts.

The prior quarter saw a deceleration in international transaction revenue growth to 6% year-over-year from 10% in the fiscal fourth quarter of 2025, impacted by lower foreign-exchange volatility, according to the research note. Management had cautioned that persistently low volumes can pose downside risks for revenues throughout the year.

Similarly, Purchase, New York-based Mastercard will report its fiscal first-quarter earnings on Thursday, April 30, before the market open.

Consensus adjusted EPS estimate stands at $4.41, vs. $4.76 recorded in the fourth quarter of 2025 and $3.73 in Q1 2025. Revenue estimates stand at $8.26B, vs. $8.81B in the previous quarter and $7.25B a year ago.

For Mastercard, the stock gained after earnings and revenue exceeded Wall Street expectations as resilient consumer spending continued to support volume on its network.

"The US-Iran conflict is expected to have a higher impact on the Middle East, Europe, and Asia vs. the U.S. in terms of travel disruption impacting cross-border volumes and the energy shock impacting demand and domestic volumes. MA is viewed as more vulnerable vs. V given its higher ex-U.S. exposure (66% vs. 51% of volumes, respectively)," said Evercore analysts.

For both stocks, cross-border volumes and revenue will be closely watched this quarter. 

Visible Alpha consensus for Q1 2026 cross-border volume growth (ex Intra-Europe) stands at 12% for Visa (vs. 11% in the prior quarter and 13% a year ago) and 13% for Mastercard (vs. 14% in the prior quarter and 15% a year ago).

data.title: Big Tech earnings test AI spending as constraints begin to surface, Citi says
data.description:
Big Tech earnings kick off this week with Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Meta (META), and Apple (AAPL) all set to report, shifting investor attention from individual results to a broader question:

Can the sector sustain its aggressive AI-driven investment cycle without eroding returns? 

Citi’s head of tech and communications research, Heath Terry, noted that while capital expenditure expectations remain high, only modest upward revisions are likely in the near term. Following a surge in spending over the past year—well above prior consensus estimates—the scale of investment reflects accelerating demand for AI infrastructure alongside shifting global dynamics.

Yet clear constraints are beginning to surface. Terry added that despite a strong willingness to deploy more capital, companies face practical limitations, including shortages in power capacity, skilled labor, and regulatory approvals. These factors could cap how quickly firms can expand, even amid intensifying competitive pressure. 

As a result, investor focus is evolving. The debate is no longer centered solely on how much companies spend but on how effectively that spending translates into growth. While revenues are expected to improve modestly, markets are increasingly demanding evidence that top-line expansion can outpace capital intensity. 

With AI investment lifting the entire sector, this earnings season will serve as a key test of whether that spending can ultimately drive durable, profitable growth, Terry said.

data.title: FanDuel, DraftKings could gain from proposed prediction market regulation
data.description:
A bipartisan effort to keep prediction markets out of sports betting gave shares of Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) and DraftKings (DKNG) a boost on Monday.

Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) is the parent company of FanDuel.

According to The Wall Street Journal, California Democrat Adam Schiff and Utah Republican John Curtis plan to introduce legislation that would regulate prediction market entities like Polymarket and Kalshi and prevent them from listing contracts for sporting events and casino-style games, thereby reducing competition for traditional sports betting offered on FanDuel (FLUT) and DraftKings (DKNG).

By leveraging their regulatory status with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission ((CFTC)), sites like Kalshi and Polymarket have been able to bypass state-by-state gaming laws that have limited the expansion of FanDuel (FLUT) and DraftKings (DKNG). The CFTC currently classifies prediction markets as derivative contracts, or swaps.

"The CFTC is greenlighting these markets and even promoting their growth," Schiff said to The Wall Street Journal, with Curtis adding that gaming contracts belong under state control, not under federal regulators.

If passed, the bill proposed by Schiff and Curtis would significantly change competition in the U.S. online gaming market by removing prediction markets as an alternative.
